LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED LARGE PINES TWISTED AND BROKEN WITH SEVERAL POWERLINES DOWN ALONG BAY VIEW RD IN BAYVIEW.

THE NWS IN NEWPORT, NC SURVEYED DAMAGE THAT OCCURRED IN BAYVIEW, NC. THE SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT A WATERSPOUT MOVED ASHORE AND TOUCHED DOWN AS A BRIEF TORNADO IN TOWN BEFORE LIFTING. THE TORNADO WAS MOVING VERY QUICKLY IN A NORTHEASTWARD DIRECTION AND AFFECTED A NEIGHBORHOOD ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE BAYVIEW GOLF CLUB. SEVERAL VERY LARGE 3 FT DIAMETER PINE TREES HAD THEIR TRUNKS SNAPPED ABOUT 12 FEET UP. THERE WAS ALSO A LARGE 2 FOOT CEDAR TREE THAT WAS SNAPPED. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE INCLUDED A LARGE SHED THAT HAD ITS TIN ROOF TORN OFF AND THROWN APPROXIMATELY 25 YARDS, SHINGLES THAT WERE TORN FROM SEVERAL HOMES, AND LARGE LIMBS THAT WERE BLOWN DOWN.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT A BRIEF EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG LESTER HOUSTON ROAD JUST TO THE EAST OF BEULAVILLE. THE TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE TO TREES AND A COUPLE OF STRUCTURES IN THE AREA.
DAMAGE TO OUTBUILDINGS AND ROOF DAMAGE TO ONE BUSINESS IN RICHLANDS.
A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT A BRIEF EFO TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTHWEST OF RICHLANDS IN ONSLOW COUNTY, WITH PEAK WINDS OF 75 MPH. THE TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE TO A FEW STRUCTURES AND A BOAT.
DELAYED REPORT. TREE DAMAGE AND DAMAGE TO A LARGE SHED AND CARPORT WERE REPORTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 41 AND COUNTRY LANE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT A BRIEF, EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN WEST OF TRENTON IN JONES COUNTY, WITH PEAK WINDS OF 90 MPH. THE TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE TO SOFTWOOD AND HARDWOOD TREES, AND CAUSED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A METAL STORAGE STRUCTURE. SOME DAMAGE WAS FOUND 20 FEET UP IN A NEARBY TREE.

A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT A BRIEF AND WEAK EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF TRENTON IN JONES COUNTY, WITH PEAK WINDS OF 85 MPH. DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH THE TORNADO WAS PRIMARILY TO TREES ALONG THE PATH.
